creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
Almost forgot. The other thing you need to be concerned about is:

source src { unix-dgram("/var/run/log");
             unix-dgram("/var/run/logpriv" perm(0600));
             udp(); internal(); file("/dev/klog"); };

These need to match up with:

#define _PATH_LOG       "/var/run/log"
#define _PATH_LOG_PRIV  "/var/run/logpriv"
#define _PATH_OLDLOG    "/dev/log"      /* backward compatibility */

Therefore the only solution is an ACL.

Also if you want syslog-ng to listen to the syslog ports:

syslog		514/udp
syslog-conn	601/tcp    #Reliable Syslog Service
syslog-conn	601/udp    #Reliable Syslog Service

It needs to run as root (unless  you're using capabilities). On all UNIX and UNIX-like systems (this includes *BSD, Linux, Solaris, AIX, SYSV, etc.) ports < 1024 can only be opened by root.
